name: Cccs Of Sheboygan
description_short: NFCC-approved nonprofit credit counseling organization serving Wisconsin and Minnesota with free financial assessments, debt management, and homeowner education.
description_long: CCCS of Sheboygan is a nonprofit financial counseling organization approved by the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC) and operates across Wisconsin and Minnesota, serving communities including Sheboygan, La Crosse, West Bend, and Fond du Lac. The organization positions itself as a comprehensive financial wellness guide, helping consumers navigate credit, debt, and housing decisions through professional counseling and educational programs. CCCS offers free one-hour initial consultations that include comprehensive financial assessments, credit score analysis, goal planning, and personalized action plans. Their services span one-on-one debt counseling, financial skills education through partner organizations, homeowner education, and ongoing counselor support after initial consultations. What distinguishes CCCS is their NFCC certification, their emphasis on educational empowerment rather than shame-based approaches (as evidenced by client testimonials), and their focus on measurable outcomes—they report that the majority of clients increase savings and decrease debt by 10% within six months. The organization operates a structured intake process (phone call or form submission, team outreach, counselor meeting, ongoing support) designed to reduce friction for consumers seeking help. However, CCCS is a traditional nonprofit credit counselor rather than a debt settlement or credit repair firm, meaning they focus on budgeting, financial planning, and structured debt management rather than disputing credit reports or negotiating with creditors. Their service area is geographically limited to specific Wisconsin and Minnesota regions, which may exclude consumers outside these areas. The website emphasizes accessibility and non-judgmental support, suggesting they serve consumers with varying financial literacy levels and debt backgrounds.
pros: NFCC-certified counselors provide credibility and adherence to professional standards
Free one-hour initial consultation with comprehensive financial assessment and credit score review
Documented client outcomes showing 10% average debt reduction and savings increase within six months
Non-judgmental educational approach—clients report feeling empowered rather than judged
Ongoing counselor support available after initial consultation, not just one-time advice
Multiple service offerings including debt counseling, financial education, and homeowner education
Free service model removes financial barriers to accessing professional financial guidance
cons: Service area limited to specific Wisconsin and Minnesota regions (Sheboygan, La Crosse, West Bend, Fond du Lac); unavailable for consumers outside these areas
Website does not clearly specify whether they offer formal debt management plans or debt consolidation services
No pricing information provided for ongoing counseling beyond the free initial consultation
Limited details about turnaround time for appointment scheduling or counselor availability
Website lacks information about debt negotiation capabilities with creditors
best_for: Wisconsin and Minnesota residents with debt, poor credit, or financial confusion seeking free professional guidance
First-time credit counseling clients who value non-judgmental education and empowerment over aggressive debt tactics
Homeowners or prospective homebuyers needing housing-specific financial counseling and education
diagnosis: CCCS of Sheboygan is best for Wisconsin and Minnesota residents seeking free, judgment-free credit counseling and financial education from a certified nonprofit. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—their services are restricted to specific Wisconsin and Minnesota regions, making them inaccessible for consumers outside these areas, and they focus on counseling and education rather than debt settlement or credit repair.
services: Free one-hour financial consultation and intake assessment
One-on-one debt counseling with certified counselors
Comprehensive financial assessment and budget planning
Credit score analysis and credit report review
Homeowner education and housing decision counseling
Ongoing counselor support for existing clients
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 505 King Street, Suite 212, , La Crosse, WI 54601
